calendar.matters:
Motion For Waiver Of Bond Requirement (Ccp995.240)
Off Calendar Nov-09-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSEPH R. LORDAN OF LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-262-8509 APPEARING FOR THE APPELLANT;
ATTY. SEAN RYAN BRODERICK OF THE HANSEN KOHLS & JACOBS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-916-781-2550 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. APPELLANTS OOMBA INC. AND OOMBA TRUST LLC'S Motion For Waiver Of Bond Requirement (CCP 995.240) is denied. Labor Code section 98.2 states that as a condition of filing an appeal an employer shall first post an undertaking with the reviewing court in the amount of the order, decision, or award. (Labor Code section 98.2.) In appealing the California Labor Commission's Division of Labor Standards Enforcement's decision, Oomba, Inc. failed to post a bond and waited until the filing of the instant motion to seek relief. Further support for denying Oomba, Inc.'s motion is found in Williams v. FreedomCard, Inc. (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 609. In Williams, the court noted that a corporate entity cannot claim to be indigent in order to obtain relief from having to post a bond under Labor Code section 98.2(b). (Williams v. FreedomCard, Inc. (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 609, 615.) However, even assuming a corporate entity can seek relief, Oomba, Inc.'s has made a "weak and incomplete showing of indigency." (Williams, supra, 123 Cal.App.4th at 614.) Finally, a weighing of the factors set out in CCP 995.240 supports denying Oomba, Inc.'s motion. California has a strong public policy in favor of protecting an employee from an employer's abuse and a granting of the motion could harm Plaintiff and her ability to recover the wages. (Kerr's Catering Serv. V. Department of Industrial Relations (1962) 57 Cal.2d 319, 326-27.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
